vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1737: feat(dashboard): merge-queue UX overhaul — branch names, honest control labels, UAT instructions + live UAT-frontend link

plan.title: merge-queue UX overhaul — branch names, honest control labels, UAT instructions + live UAT-frontend link
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The Flywheel MergeQueueCard has misleading controls and missing context. (1) The action button reads 'Ship batch' but actually calls /api/flywheel/merge-next, which sequentially merges N queue items to main with full ceremony and stops on the first failure — the label hides what it does. (2) The assemble control (calls /api/flywheel/assemble-uat to build the UAT-candidate branch) only appears when batch-safe items exist, so with an all-serialize queue the operator never learns the capability or why it is unavailable. (3) Rows show only the issue id — no feature branch name and no clickable PR link. (4) Before merging, the operator must UAT but the card never says WHAT to test. (5) There is no one-click path to a running frontend serving the work under test.
narratives.Proposal: Overhaul the card in place using existing dashboard primitives and a trivial backend enrichment. (a) Make controls honest: rename the assemble control to 'Assemble UAT branch' and render it always — disabled with an explanatory tooltip when there are no batch-safe items; drop the 'Ship batch' label, label the merge action 'Merge N to main', and gate it behind a useConfirm() dialog (the MergeButton.tsx pattern) whose message states it merges N queue items to main in order and stops on the first failure. (b) Enrich the backend MergeQueueItem with branchName (already computed as feature/<id>) and prUrl, and render feature/pan-XXXX plus a clickable PR link per row. (c) Add a collapsible 'What to UAT' section for the queue head sourced from the SAME vBRIEF acceptance-criteria extractor PAN-1686 uses (src/lib/vbrief/acceptance-criteria.ts via GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/plan) — never a new parser. (d) Give each ready row a one-click link to its existing workspace frontendUrl for live UAT (reusing /api/workspaces/:issueId, the AwaitingMergePage pattern). (e) Reorganize the card into three clear zones (UAT candidate / serialized queue / controls). Spinning a UNIFIED preview stack for the assembled uat/<codename> candidate branch is a separate ~300-500 LOC infra subsystem with docker-network and single-deacon hazards; it is split into a follow-up issue, not built here.

autoDecisions.summary: Split the assembled-UAT-candidate unified preview stack (a running frontend serving the uat/<codename> branch) into follow-up issue PAN-1738; PAN-1737 delivers the per-feature live frontend link using each ready issue's existing workspace stack.
autoDecisions.rationale: Spinning a stack for an arbitrary uat/* branch requires new infra (worktree + devcontainer render + docker compose up + Traefik host, ~300-500 LOC) and carries real hazards (Docker's ~31-network bridge pool exhaustion documented in CLAUDE.md; the single-deacon invariant). It is cleanly separable from a frontend card-UX overhaul. Linking each ready feature to its already-running workspace frontend delivers the bulk of the UAT value with zero new infra and no dead UI, satisfying the no-partial-implementation rule.

autoDecisions.summary: Source UAT guidance from the existing GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/plan endpoint plus the shared src/lib/vbrief/acceptance-criteria.ts extractor, rather than hard-depending on PAN-1686's in-flight /api/workspaces/:issueId/uat-context endpoint.
autoDecisions.rationale: The shared extractor is already on main; the /uat-context endpoint lives on the unmerged feature/pan-1686 branch. Reusing the extractor shares the data source (the issue's explicit ask — 'same data source, don't duplicate') without blocking PAN-1737 on another branch. If /uat-context has merged by implementation time, consume it instead.

autoDecisions.summary: Render the 'Assemble UAT branch' control always — disabled with a tooltip when no batch-safe items exist — instead of hiding it.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue explicitly asks for disable-with-tooltip so the operator learns the capability and understands why it is unavailable on an all-serialize queue, rather than seeing a silently-absent button.

autoDecisions.summary: Use the existing useConfirm() dialog (DialogProvider) for the merge-to-main confirmation, matching MergeButton.tsx, rather than window.confirm.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps the dashboard's confirm-dialog convention consistent and themable; MergeButton.tsx is the exact merge-to-main analog.

items.title: Backend: add branchName + prUrl to MergeQueueItem and return from /api/flywheel/merge-queue

narrative.Action: Enrich the MergeQueueItem contract in src/lib/flywheel-merge-order.ts so the frontend does not re-derive branch names or construct PR URLs in the browser. Add branchName (already computed internally as feature/<issueId lowercased> near line 119) and an optional prUrl resolved server-side (reuse the existing review-status prUrl / workspace mrUrl resolution the dashboard already uses, or construct from the issue's GitHub repo slug via resolveProjectFromIssue + the pr number). Return both fields from GET /api/flywheel/merge-queue (src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts ~line 892). Leave all existing fields unchanged.

subItems.title: MergeQueueItem gains branchName: string, populated as feature/<issueId lowercased> for every item; existing flywheel-merge-order unit tests updated/added to assert it.
MergeQueueItem gains optional prUrl?: string resolved server-side; present whenever a PR exists for the issue, absent otherwise. No browser-side repo-slug guessing required.
GET /api/flywheel/merge-queue response includes branchName and prUrl on each item; all previously-returned fields are unchanged; typecheck/lint/tests pass.

items.title: Frontend: rename assemble control to 'Assemble UAT branch' + disable-with-tooltip when no batch-safe items

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/MergeQueueCard.tsx, make the assemble control honest. Rename it to 'Assemble UAT branch' and render it ALWAYS (move it out of the candidate-box-only conditional) so it is visible even on an all-serialize queue, in a disabled state with a tooltip explaining why (e.g. 'No batch-safe items to bundle — all ready items must merge one at a time'). When batch-safe items exist, clicking still POSTs /api/flywheel/assemble-uat exactly as today. Keep the assemble action visually distinct from the merge-to-main action so it never reads as a merge.

subItems.title: The control's label reads 'Assemble UAT branch' (no bare 'Assemble', no 'Ship batch').

When uat-candidate has no batch-safe items, the control is shown but disabled, with a tooltip stating no batch-safe items exist; when items exist it is enabled and POSTs /api/flywheel/assemble-uat as before.
The assemble control is visually distinct from the merge-to-main action (different placement/treatment) so it cannot be mistaken for a merge.

items.title: Frontend: replace 'Ship batch' with 'Merge N to main' + confirm dialog stating exact semantics

narrative.Action: In MergeQueueCard.tsx, drop the misleading 'Ship batch' label on the merge-next action button. Label it 'Merge {n} to main' (correct singular/plural). Before firing mergeNextMutation, open a useConfirm() dialog (import from DialogProvider, matching MergeButton.tsx) whose message states exactly what happens: it merges {n} queue items to main, in order, and stops on the first failure. Confirming triggers merge-next; cancelling does nothing. Keep the existing N stepper and the existing toast handling.

subItems.title: The merge action button no longer contains the text 'Ship batch'; it reads 'Merge {n} to main' with correct pluralization.

Clicking the button opens a useConfirm() dialog whose message states it merges {n} queue items to main in order and stops on the first failure; confirming POSTs /api/flywheel/merge-next with n, cancelling makes no request.
Uses the existing useConfirm hook from DialogProvider (no window.confirm); existing stepper and toast-on-result behavior preserved.

items.title: Frontend: show feature/pan-XXXX branch name + clickable PR link on every queue row

narrative.Action: In MergeQueueCard.tsx, render the branch name and a clickable PR link on each queue row (both the candidate-bundled rows and the serialized rows). Use item.branchName (from the enriched backend item) shown in monospace alongside the issue id. Where item.prUrl exists, render a clickable link that opens the PR in a new tab (mirror AwaitingMergePage's prUrl link treatment); the existing bare #pr number can become that link's label. Preserve the existing IdLink navigate-to-issue behavior.

subItems.title: Every queue row (candidate-bundled and serialized) displays its branch name feature/pan-XXXX in monospace, taken from item.branchName.
Rows with a PR show a clickable link (target=_blank, rel=noreferrer) to item.prUrl; rows without a PR omit the link gracefully.
The existing IdLink (click issue id → onNavigateIssue) behavior is unchanged.

items.title: Frontend: collapsible 'What to UAT' section for the queue head, sourced from the shared vBRIEF AC extractor (no duplication of PAN-1686)

narrative.Action: Add a collapsible 'What to UAT' section to MergeQueueCard.tsx for the queue head (the next item to merge — mergeOrder 1). Populate it with that issue's acceptance criteria pulled from the vBRIEF. CRITICAL: reuse the SAME data source PAN-1686 uses — fetch GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/plan and extract criteria with the shared extractor in src/lib/vbrief/acceptance-criteria.ts (extractACFromDocument / extractAcceptanceCriteriaSync). Do NOT add a new vBRIEF-parsing path. If PAN-1686's GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/uat-context endpoint has merged by implementation time, prefer consuming it. Fetch lazily (on expand or for the head only) to avoid N+1 queries.

subItems.title: The card shows a collapsible 'What to UAT' section for the queue head listing that issue's acceptance criteria from the vBRIEF.

Acceptance criteria are obtained via GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/plan + the shared src/lib/vbrief/acceptance-criteria.ts extractor (or /uat-context if present); NO new vBRIEF-parsing code is introduced — verifiable from the diff.
Graceful fallback: when the plan has no acceptance criteria, the section shows the issue title/proposal or a 'No UAT steps in plan' note rather than erroring or rendering empty.

items.title: Frontend: one-click 'Open frontend' link to each ready issue's existing workspace frontendUrl for live UAT

narrative.Action: Give each queue row (at minimum the queue head) a one-click link to that issue's running workspace frontend so the operator can exercise the change before merging. Reuse the existing pattern: fetch /api/workspaces/:issueId and read frontendUrl (the same field AwaitingMergePage uses). Render an 'Open frontend' link (target=_blank) only when frontendUrl is available; otherwise show a disabled/absent affordance with an explanatory tooltip (mirror AwaitingMergePage's `frontendUrl ? link : disabled` handling). Use the established per-issue workspace fetch (e.g. useQueries) bounded by queue size — no new backend endpoint. NOTE: this covers the per-feature live frontend; a unified preview stack for the assembled uat/<codename> candidate is the split-out follow-up issue, not this bead.

subItems.title: The queue head (and each row) exposes a one-click 'Open frontend' link to that issue's workspace frontendUrl, opening in a new tab.
The link renders only when a frontendUrl is available; when absent, a disabled/absent affordance with a tooltip is shown (mirroring AwaitingMergePage). No errors on missing workspace data.
Implemented by reusing the existing /api/workspaces/:issueId fetch pattern (no new backend route); requests are bounded by queue size.

items.title: Frontend: reorganize the card into three clear zones (UAT candidate / serialized queue / controls)

narrative.Action: Final pass over MergeQueueCard.tsx layout: establish a clear visual hierarchy with three zones — (1) the UAT candidate group, (2) the serialized queue, (3) the controls (Assemble UAT branch + Merge to main), with adequate spacing and headers rather than the current stacked elements. This is presentation only: do not drop any action or data added by the prior beads (branch names, PR links, What-to-UAT section, live-frontend links, both controls). Keep it readable at the rail width.

subItems.title: The card presents three visually distinct zones — UAT candidate, serialized queue, and controls — with clear headers/spacing and no stacked-banner glob.

No actions or data from prior beads are removed: branch names, PR links, the What-to-UAT section, live-frontend links, and both the Assemble and Merge-to-main controls all remain present and functional.
Visual verification (isolated Playwright profile / screenshot) confirms the card renders without overflow or truncation regressions at the rail width.
